sources.thermalPaste: Verified from Noctua installation manuals, Arctic MX mounting guides, and Cooler Master instructions. Pea-sized method is the universally recommended application for direct-die contact coolers.
sources.buildOrder: Standard PC assembly sequence per form factor, verified from GamersNexus build guides and r/buildapc wiki. Mini-ITX requires PSU-first due to restricted cable access.
sources.toolChecklist: Standard PC building tool set verified from iFixit Pro Tech Toolkit contents and r/buildapc wiki.
sources.psuInstallation: Bottom-mount PSU fan orientation standards per case manufacturer manuals (Fractal Design, Corsair, NZXT).
sources.gpuSag: GPU sag risk based on known physical characteristics: cards exceeding 300mm length and premium tier (ultra/halo) weight. Verified from GamersNexus GPU sag testing.
sources.buildTime: Time estimates based on averaged self-reported build times from r/buildapc community surveys (2024-2026).

thermalPastePatterns.pattern: pea-sized dot applied to center of CPU integrated heat spreader (IHS)
thermalPastePatterns.rationale: Air tower coolers with direct-contact heat pipes or lapped base plates distribute pressure evenly across the IHS. A pea-sized dot (3-4mm diameter) spreads to full coverage without excess squeeze-out.

thermalPastePatterns.pattern: pea-sized dot applied to center of CPU IHS
thermalPastePatterns.rationale: AIO cold plates apply even pressure across the socket. A center pea method prevents air pockets and ensures complete coverage without paste spilling over the IHS edges.

thermalPastePatterns.pattern: pea-sized dot applied to center of CPU IHS
thermalPastePatterns.rationale: Same application method as 240mm AIOs — cold plate design is identical. Center pea method provides optimal thermal transfer for 360mm radiators.

thermalPastePatterns.pattern: pea-sized dot applied to center of CPU IHS
thermalPastePatterns.rationale: Low-profile coolers use the same mounting pressure mechanism as full-size air towers. A smaller pea (2-3mm) may be sufficient for smaller IHS contact areas.
Boxed coolers from AMD and Intel include pre-applied thermal compound. Do not add additional paste. If replacing the cooler, clean the IHS with isopropyl alcohol first.
thermalPastePatterns.source: AMD Wraith cooler documentation, Intel stock cooler guide

thermalPastePatterns.pattern: thin even spread across the entire cold plate surface
thermalPastePatterns.rationale: Custom water blocks use a spread method to ensure complete, even coverage across the larger cold plate area. Use a plastic spreader or credit-card edge to apply an thin, uniform layer.

steps.detail: Mount PSU in bottom chamber, fan facing downward (intake from bottom vent). Route EPS/ATX cables through nearest grommet before securing with screws.
Open socket lever, align CPU triangle marker with socket triangle, gently lower CPU, close lever. No force required — CPU should drop into place.
Pop open DIMM slot clips. Align RAM notch with slot key, press firmly until clips click. Install in slots A2/B2 (2nd and 4th from CPU) for dual-channel.
Insert M.2 drive at 30-degree angle into slot, press down, secure with motherboard-supplied screw.
Apply thermal paste (see paste section). Mount cooler bracket per manufacturer instructions. Tighten screws in cross pattern (star pattern) to ensure even pressure.
Verify standoffs align with motherboard mounting holes. Place I/O shield in case first. Lower motherboard carefully, align with standoffs, secure with screws in cross pattern.
Front panel header (power switch, reset, LEDs), USB 3.0 internal header, audio header, case fan connectors. Refer to motherboard manual for exact pin layout.
Remove PCIe slot covers on case rear. Align GPU with PCIe x16 slot, press firmly until retention clip clicks. Secure GPU bracket to case with screws.
24-pin ATX to motherboard. 8-pin (4+4) EPS to CPU top-left. PCIe cables to GPU — use separate cables for each connector, do not daisy-chain for high-power cards.
Route excess cables behind motherboard tray. Secure with zip ties or Velcro straps. Ensure cables do not block case fan airflow or GPU fans.
Mount case fans in intake (front typically) and exhaust (rear typically) positions. Verify fan orientation — side with frame arms is exhaust, open side is intake.
Verify all connections are fully seated. Check for loose screws or tools inside case. Connect monitor to GPU (not motherboard). Power on at PSU switch first, then case power button.
In SFF cases, install PSU first as cable access is restricted once motherboard is installed. PSU position varies by case (front bracket, bottom, or top).
steps.source: Mini-ITX case manuals (Cooler Master NR200P, NZXT H1)

steps.detail: Same procedure as ATX: open socket, align triangle marker, drop CPU, close lever.
ITX boards have 2 DIMM slots. Install in slot closest to CPU or per motherboard manual.
M.2 slot is often on the front of ITX boards. Install before CPU cooler if cooler overhangs the M.2 area.
Apply thermal paste (see paste section). Verify cooler height fits case — SFF cases have strict height limits. Low-profile coolers require specific mounting.
Slide or lower motherboard into case carefully. SFF cases may require angled insertion. Secure with provided screws.
Route cables before installing GPU. Use custom-length cables or manage excess carefully in confined space.
Install GPU last as it often blocks cable access in SFF cases. Use riser cable if GPU is vertically mounted (some SFF cases).
Route front panel and fan cables around GPU. Use zip ties to secure cables against case frame.
Verify GPU is fully seated and no cables contact fans. Power on and verify POST.
PSU installs in bottom chamber. Full towers offer more cable routing space behind motherboard tray. Route all needed cables before securing.
Standard socket installation procedure.
Dual-channel in slots A2/B2. Full tower cases provide unobstructed access.
Standard M.2 installation.
Large tower coolers fit easily in full towers (185mm+ clearance). Standard mounting procedure.
Full towers have pre-installed standoffs. Extra space makes motherboard installation straightforward.
Standard front panel, USB, and audio connections.
Full towers accommodate even the longest GPUs (up to 470mm). Remove required PCIe slot covers and secure GPU.
Route cables behind motherboard tray. Full towers offer generous cable management space with Velcro straps.
Full towers support up to 10+ fans. Install in front intake, top intake/exhaust, rear exhaust, and bottom intake configuration.
Use the extensive cable routing channels and Velcro straps provided in full tower cases.
Verify all connections. POST the system.

toolChecklist.rationale: Used for motherboard, PSU, GPU, and drive mounting screws

toolChecklist.tool: Zip ties or Velcro cable straps
toolChecklist.rationale: Cable management inside case to improve airflow
Cutting zip tie tails flush after cable management

toolChecklist.tool: Anti-static wrist strap (or touch unpainted metal regularly)
toolChecklist.rationale: Prevents electrostatic discharge damage to components

toolChecklist.tool: Isopropyl alcohol (90%+) + coffee filter or lint-free cloth
toolChecklist.rationale: Cleaning old thermal paste from CPU if reapplying
Illuminating interior of case during assembly

toolChecklist.tool: Small bowl or magnetic mat for screws
toolChecklist.rationale: Keeps screws organized by size and type

mid-tower.fanDirection: Fan faces DOWN (intake from bottom case vent)
mid-tower.note: If case has no bottom vent (some older cases), fan faces UP. Verify your case has a bottom air filter.

mini-itx.note: NR200P: PSU mounts on front bracket. NZXT H1: PSU is pre-installed in spine. Always refer to case manual for SFF PSU placement.

full-tower.fanDirection: Fan faces DOWN (intake from bottom case vent)
full-tower.note: Full towers have dedicated PSU intake with dust filter on bottom panel.

gpuSagRule.recommendation: Graphics cards in the premium/halo tier that exceed 300mm in length benefit from a GPU support bracket or anti-sag stand to prevent PCIe slot damage and GPU PCB bending over time.
gpuSagRule.source: Verified from GamersNexus GPU sag investigation

buildTimeEstimates.rangeNote: Times are estimates based on first-time builder surveys. Experienced builders complete in 30-50% less time.
